rag_rules: Preserve owner scope when fusing: component type contracts belong on component types; implementation-level bindings belong on component implementations.
Use the parsed AADL model as the authority for owner names, features, subcomponents, connections, and existing structure before inserting annex text.
generic_rules: Apply the smallest possible textual change and preserve all existing architecture, declarations, and unrelated content.
Fuse annex content only into an existing valid owner; do not create new structural elements or infer missing model structure.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Annex content uses the standard AADL wrapper `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Implementation-scope content should appear only where the needed structural context is already declared.
anti_patterns: Do not place type-scope contract text in an implementation owner, or implementation-only content in a component type owner.
Do not alter features, connections, properties, or subcomponent structure just to accommodate annex insertion.
irrelevant_topics: Numeric range handling for Boolean or opaque data
Treating AADL property names or unit tokens as in-scope variables
